summary.text:
**Computing summary statistics**

I’m thinking about how to summarize some data, possibly comparing the first 45 entries to the last 45, or maybe looking at the regression slope. It seems like I need to compute some summary statistics to better understand what’s going on. This could provide insights into any trends or changes within the data set. Let’s get started on figuring out those stats!

output.arguments:
{"query":"with max_date as (
  select max(digitalbankinglogintransaction_timestamp)::date as max_dt
  from fact.digitalbankinglogintransaction
), dates as (
  select generate_series((select max_dt - interval '89 days' from max_date),
                         (select max_dt from max_date),
                         interval '1 day')::date as login_date
), daily as (
  select d.login_date,
         coalesce(sum(coalesce(nullif(f.digitalbankinglogintransaction_count,'')::numeric,1)),0) as login_count
  from dates d
  left join fact.digitalbankinglogintransaction f
    on f.digitalbankinglogintransaction_timestamp::date = d.login_date
  group by d.login_date
), stats as (
  select count(*) as days,
         sum(login_count) as total_logins,
         round(avg(login_count),2) as avg_daily_logins,
         min(login_count) as min_daily_logins,
         max(login_count) as max_daily_logins,
         round(regr_slope(login_count, rn),4) as slope_per_day
  from (
    select login_date, login_count,
           row_number() over (order by login_date) as rn
    from daily
  ) x
), halves as (
  select case when row_number() over (order by login_date) <= 45 then 'first_45' else 'last_45' end as period,
         avg(login_count) as avg_logins,
         sum(login_count) as total_logins
  from daily
  group by 1
), weekly as (
  select date_trunc('week', login_date)::date as week_start,
         sum(login_count) as weekly_logins
  from daily
  group by 1
  order by 1
)
select json_build_object(
  'stats', (select row_to_json(stats) from stats),
  'halves', (select json_agg(row_to_json(halves)) from halves),
  'weekly', (select json_agg(row_to_json(weekly)) from weekly)
) as result;"}

{"query":"with max_date as (
  select max(digitalbankinglogintransaction_timestamp)::date as max_dt
  from fact.digitalbankinglogintransaction
), dates as (
  select generate_series((select max_dt - interval '89 days' from max_date),
                         (select max_dt from max_date),
                         interval '1 day')::date as login_date
), daily as (
  select d.login_date,
         coalesce(sum(case when f.digitalbankinglogintransaction_timestamp is not null
                           then coalesce(nullif(f.digitalbankinglogintransaction_count,'')::numeric,1)
                           else 0 end),0) as login_count
  from dates d
  left join fact.digitalbankinglogintransaction f
    on f.digitalbankinglogintransaction_timestamp::date = d.login_date
  group by d.login_date
), daily_ranked as (
  select login_date, login_count,
         row_number() over (order by login_date) as rn
  from daily
), stats as (
  select count(*) as days,
         sum(login_count) as total_logins,
         round(avg(login_count),2) as avg_daily_logins,
         min(login_count) as min_daily_logins,
         max(login_count) as max_daily_logins,
         round((regr_slope(login_count, rn))::numeric,4) as slope_per_day
  from daily_ranked
), halves as (
  select case when rn <= 45 then 'first_45' else 'last_45' end as period,
         round(avg(login_count),2) as avg_logins,
         sum(login_count) as total_logins
  from daily_ranked
  group by 1
), weekly as (
  select date_trunc('week', login_date)::date as week_start,
         sum(login_count) as weekly_logins
  from daily
  group by 1
  order by 1
)
select json_build_object(
  'stats', (select row_to_json(stats) from stats),
  'halves', (select json_agg(row_to_json(halves)) from halves),
  'weekly', (select json_agg(row_to_json(weekly)) from weekly)
) as result;"}
